content.problemStatement:
Chef is playing a game on the **non-negative** x-axis. It takes him 111 second to reach from ithi^{th}ith position to (i‚ąí1)th(i-1)^{th}(i‚ąí1)th position or (i+1)th(i+1)^{th}(i+1)th position. The chef never goes to the negative x-axis. Also, Chef doesn't stop at any moment of time.

The movement of chef can be described as follows.


At the start he is standing at x=0x=0x=0 at time 000.
In the first round, he moves towards x=1x=1x=1 and comes back to the x=0x=0x=0 position.
In the second round, he moves towards the x=2x=2x=2 and comes back again to x=0x=0x=0.
Generalizing, in the kthk^{th}kth round, he moves from x=0x=0x=0 to x=kx=kx=k and then returns back to x=0x=0x=0 at the end of the round. This goes on as the game progresses.
For Example, the path of Chef for 3rd3^{rd}3rd round is given below.

0‚ąí1‚ąí2‚ąí3‚ąí2‚ąí1‚ąí00 - 1 - 2 - 3 - 2 - 1 - 00‚ąí1‚ąí2‚ąí3‚ąí2‚ąí1‚ąí0

The overall path followed by Chef would look somewhat like this:

0‚ąí1‚ąí0‚ąí1‚ąí2‚ąí1‚ąí0‚ąí1‚ąí2‚ąí3‚ąí2‚ąí1‚ąí0‚ąí1‚ąí2‚ąí3‚ąí4‚ąí3‚ąí‚Ä¶0 - 1 - 0 - 1 - 2 - 1 - 0 - 1 - 2 - 3 - 2 - 1 - 0 - 1 - 2 - 3 - 4 - 3 - ‚Ä¶0‚ąí1‚ąí0‚ąí1‚ąí2‚ąí1‚ąí0‚ąí1‚ąí2‚ąí3‚ąí2‚ąí1‚ąí0‚ąí1‚ąí2‚ąí3‚ąí4‚ąí3‚ąí‚Ä¶

You are given two **non-negative** integers NNN and KKK. You have to tell **the time** at which Chef arrives at x=Nx=Nx=N for the KthK^{th}Kth time.

**Note - Chef can not skip a position while visiting the positions.**

###Input:


The first line contains TTT the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of two integers NNN and KKK.
###Output:
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer -- the time taken by the chef to arrive at x=Nx=Nx=N for the KthK^{th}Kth time by modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007.

###Constraints


1‚Č§T‚Č§1051 \le T \le 10^51‚Č§T‚Č§105
0‚Č§N‚Č§1090 \le N \le 10^90‚Č§N‚Č§109
1‚Č§K‚Č§1091 \le K \le 10^91‚Č§K‚Č§109

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:
Chef starts the journey from the N=0N = 0N=0 at time t=0t = 0t=0 and it's the first time (K=1)(K = 1)(K=1), he is here. So, the answer is 000.
Test Case 2:
Chef starts the journey from the N=0N = 0N=0 at time t=0t = 0t=0 then goes to N=1N = 1N=1 at t=1t = 1t=1 and it's the first time (K=1)(K = 1)(K=1), he is here. So, the answer is 111.
Test Case 4:
The path followed by Chef to reach 111 for the third time is given below.
0‚ąí1‚ąí0‚ąí1‚ąí2‚ąí10 - 1 - 0 - 1 - 2 - 10‚ąí1‚ąí0‚ąí1‚ąí2‚ąí1
He reaches 111 for the third time at t=5t=5t=5.
